extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial high peak and decline (first ~15% of the series), the signal reaches a clear minimum and then shows a long, sustained upward trend starting roughly in the middle of the series (from about 30–40% to 80–90% of the time).
R2: In that upward-trend region, the values typically rise from around 0.1–0.2 up to at least about 0.45–0.5.
R3: Near the very end, the series remains at medium-to-high values (around 0.4–0.7) without a pronounced overall downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial high peak and decline (first ~15% of the series), the signal continues to drift downward and reaches its minimum somewhat later than in Class 0, often around 25–35% of the series length.
R2: Following this minimum, the series increases to medium values (around 0.4–0.5) but then tends to stay roughly flat or gently decrease rather than forming a long, strong upward ramp.
R3: In the final part of the series (last ~20%), there is no sustained high plateau; the values remain in a medium range (roughly 0.35–0.5) or show a short, sharp spike that quickly drops again.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series shows a clear long rising trend starting roughly in the middle (around time 35–45) instead of earlier.
R2: The main rising segment from the minimum to its following local maximum extends over a relatively long duration (gradual climb).
R3: The final part of the series does not return to the earlier high peak level; it stays in a mid-range (around 0.4–0.6) or briefly exceeds it only near the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop, the series starts its main rising trend earlier, roughly before time 35.
R2: The rise from the minimum to its following local maximum is steeper and more concentrated in time (short, sharp climb).
R3: If there is a late peak near the end, it is reached quickly after a relatively flat mid-section, forming a pronounced “late spike” rather than a long gradual rise.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial peak and decline, the series shows a clear valley around the middle (roughly indices 30–40) with values near 0.1–0.2, then rises steadily afterwards.  
R2: In the last third of the series, the values stay mostly between 0.4 and 0.55, without a sharp spike above about 0.65.  
R3: The smallest value in the entire series is reached around the middle, not near the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series also has an early peak and decline, but the deepest valley near the middle (around indices 30–40) goes very low (close to 0 or slightly above).  
R2: After this valley, the series rises to a plateau around 0.45–0.55, then either stays roughly at that level or slowly decreases.  
R3: In the last third of the series, there is no long segment stably above about 0.5; the values stay mostly below or around 0.45.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop, the series rises from a low near 0.1–0.2 around the middle (x≈30–40) up to at least about 0.5 before the end.  
R2: In the last third of the series, values stay mostly in a higher band (roughly 0.4–0.7) without returning to the earlier minimum.  
R3: The final segment does not contain a sharp spike that clearly exceeds all previous values; it ends at a level comparable to its local highs.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop, the series rises from a low near 0.1–0.2 around the middle (x≈30–40) up to about 0.45–0.55.  
R2: In the last third of the series, values are relatively stable in a mid band (roughly 0.4–0.5), without a sustained high plateau above 0.6.  
R3: The final segment does not continue rising strongly; it is flat or slightly decreasing, and any late spike is brief and not followed by a sustained high level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series reaches its global minimum roughly in the middle of the time axis (around indices 30–40), not near the start or the end.  
R2: From that mid-series minimum, the values rise steadily toward the last third of the series, producing a clear upward trend in the second half.  
R3: The amplitude of this late upward trend is relatively large, with the maximum value occurring in the last third of the series and reaching clearly above 0.5.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series also has an early high peak followed by a decline, but its global minimum occurs earlier than in Class 0, roughly in the first third of the time axis (before indices ~30).  
R2: After this early minimum, the series increases and then flattens into a broad plateau in the middle of the time axis (indices ~35–70) with moderate oscillations but no strong upward surge.  
R3: The end of the series (last third) does not show a sustained, strong upward trend: values remain roughly around a mid-range level (about 0.4–0.5) without a late, dominant peak.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop (between time 0–20), the series eventually climbs to a relatively high late peak (time 70–95) that is clearly higher than the middle plateau (time 40–60).  
R2: In the middle part (time 40–60), values form a moderate plateau or gentle rise, staying roughly between 0.35 and 0.55.  
R3: The very end of the series (last 5–10 points) remains at a medium–to–high level and does not decay back toward the earlier minimum.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop (between time 0–20), the series rises to a middle plateau/peak (time 40–60) that is the highest sustained region of the sequence.  
R2: In the late part (time 70–95), the series either stays at or below the middle plateau or shows only a short, sharp spike that immediately falls back.  
R3: The final values (last 5–10 points) are at a medium or medium–low level, clearly below any late spike and not forming a high sustained peak.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series shows a clear upward trend from roughly the first third (around index 30–40) to about index 70.  
R2: In that upward-trend region, the values climb from a low below about 0.2 up to at least about 0.45–0.5.  
R3: In the last 20–25% of the series, values stay mostly in a mid-to-high band (roughly 0.4–0.7) without a long, steady decline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop, the series has a long mid-section (roughly index 30–70) that is relatively flat or gently varying, without a strong overall upward trend.  
R2: In that mid-section, values hover in a narrow band around 0.4–0.5, without climbing steadily from a deep low below 0.2 up to above 0.5.  
R3: If there is a strong rise late in the series (e.g., near the end), it is short and localized, not a sustained upward trend starting from the early-middle part of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop, the series shows a clear, sustained increase from its minimum up to at least around the midpoint (rising “U” shape in the middle).  
R2: In the last third of the series, values remain moderate (roughly between 0.35 and 0.55) without a sharp spike above about 0.65.  
R3: The global maximum is not at the very end of the series (the last 10–15% of time is lower or similar to earlier peaks, not the highest point).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop to a minimum around the first third, the series rises to a plateau in the middle and then shows a gentle downward or flat trend (no clear “U” shape rising through the midpoint).  
R2: In the last third of the series, there is either a noticeable local maximum (a bump) or the highest value of the whole series occurs near the end.  
R3: The global maximum is typically at the early part (first 10–20%) or at the very end, not in the central part of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series rises to a clear late peak where values exceed 0.6 near the end of the time axis.  
R2: The minimum value occurs roughly in the middle third of the series, staying above 0.05.  
R3: In the last third of the series, values fluctuate around a mid–high level (about 0.4–0.5) rather than steadily decreasing.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a strong early peak above 0.8 very close to the start, followed by a sustained decline.  
R2: The lowest point (below about 0.1–0.15) occurs relatively early to mid-series, and afterwards the series recovers only once to a moderate level (around 0.5).  
R3: The last third of the series lacks a pronounced late peak; values remain roughly flat or slightly decreasing around 0.4–0.5.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial drop, the series has a clear rising trend from around the middle (≈time 35–40) toward the end.  
R2: In the last third of the series, values stay mostly at or above a mid‑level plateau (≈0.4–0.5) without a long, steady decline.  
R3: Any sharp peak near the very end is immediately preceded by already elevated values (no long low “valley” right before the final peak).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial drop, the series stays low until roughly a third of the way through, then rises into a broad mid‑series hump.  
R2: Following this mid‑series hump, there is a gradual, overall downward or flat trend rather than a sustained late rise.  
R3: If a sharp peak appears near the very end, it is preceded by a relatively low segment (a “valley”) rather than by already high plateau values.
